supplementEntry.name: Urolithin A
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Multiple RCTs (ATLAS, ENERGIZE trials) in high-impact journals (JAMA Network Open, Cell Reports Medicine, Nature Aging) plus systematic review of 5 human studies
mechanismsOfAction: Lysosomal function restoration via cathepsin Z regulation

drugInteractions: No significant drug interactions reported in clinical trials

effectSizes.muscleStrength: ~12% improvement in hamstring muscle strength vs placebo (ATLAS trial, p<0.05)
effectSizes.muscleEndurance: Significant improvement in hand and leg muscle endurance at 2 months (ENERGIZE trial)
effectSizes.inflammation: Significant reduction in CRP levels vs placebo
effectSizes.immuneFunction: Expanded naive CD8+ T cells, increased NK cells (Nature Aging 2025 RCT)

keyCitations.title: Effect of Urolithin A Supplementation on Muscle Endurance and Mitochondrial Health in Older Adults: A Randomized Clinical Trial
Urolithin A improves muscle strength, exercise performance, and biomarkers of mitochondrial health in a randomized trial in middle-aged adults
The mitophagy activator urolithin A is safe and induces a molecular signature of improved mitochondrial and cellular health in humans
Targeting aging with urolithin A in humans: A systematic review
Effect of the mitophagy inducer urolithin A on age-related immune decline: a randomized, placebo-controlled trial

evidence.title: Urolithin A induces mitophagy and prolongs lifespan in C. elegans and increases muscle function in rodents
evidence.findings: Identified urolithin A as a first-in-class natural compound that induces mitophagy. Extended lifespan in C. elegans and improved exercise capacity in aged rodents via PINK1/Parkin-dependent mitophagy.
evidence.methodology: C. elegans lifespan assays, rodent exercise capacity testing, mitophagy fluorescence imaging

evidence.title: The mitophagy activator urolithin A is safe and induces a molecular signature of improved mitochondrial and cellular health in humans
evidence.findings: First-in-human trial demonstrated safety at all doses tested (250-2000 mg). 500 mg and 1000 mg doses modulated plasma acylcarnitines and upregulated mitochondrial gene expression in skeletal muscle.
evidence.methodology: First-in-human dose-escalation study in healthy sedentary elderly volunteers

evidence.title: Urolithin A improves muscle strength, exercise performance, and biomarkers of mitochondrial health in a randomized trial in middle-aged adults
evidence.findings: 4-month RCT (ATLAS trial) in middle-aged adults showed decreased plasma CRP and acylcarnitines, indicating anti-inflammatory and improved mitochondrial fat oxidation.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT, 4-month duration, biomarker analysis

evidence.title: Urolithin A improves Alzheimer's disease cognition and restores mitophagy and lysosomal functions
evidence.findings: In three mouse AD models, long-term UA treatment improved learning, memory, and olfactory function while reducing amyloid-beta and tau pathology. Restored lysosomal function via cathepsin Z regulation.
evidence.methodology: Three transgenic AD mouse models, behavioral testing, neuropathology assessment

mechanisms.tissueTarget: CD8+ T cells and NK cells
mechanisms.target: CD8+ T cells and NK cells

evidence.title: Effect of the mitophagy inducer urolithin A on age-related immune decline: a randomized, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: RCT in 50 middle-aged adults showed UA (1000 mg/day, 4 weeks) expanded naive-like CD8+ T cells, increased fatty acid oxidation in CD8+ cells, elevated NK cells and nonclassical monocytes. Single-cell RNA-seq confirmed metabolic reprogramming.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT with single-cell RNA sequencing immune profiling

benefits.specificClaim: Improves muscle strength and endurance in middle-aged and older adults
benefits.replicationStatus: Replicated across ATLAS and ENERGIZE trials

evidence.title: Effect of Urolithin A Supplementation on Muscle Endurance and Mitochondrial Health in Older Adults: A Randomized Clinical Trial
evidence.findings: ENERGIZE trial: UA 1000 mg/day for 4 months significantly improved muscle endurance in hand and leg muscles at 2 months vs placebo in adults mean age 71.7. Reduced inflammatory and cellular stress biomarkers.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial in older adults

evidence.title: Urolithin A improves muscle strength, exercise performance, and biomarkers of mitochondrial health in a randomized trial in middle-aged adults
evidence.findings: ATLAS trial: ~12% improvement in hamstring muscle strength vs placebo. Decreased plasma acylcarnitines and CRP, increased mitophagy-related proteins in skeletal muscle biopsies.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial in middle-aged overweight adults

benefits.specificClaim: Reverses age-related immune decline and improves T cell function

evidence.title: Effect of the mitophagy inducer urolithin A on age-related immune decline: a randomized, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Expanded naive-like, less exhausted CD8+ T cells, increased CD8+ fatty acid oxidation capacity, elevated NK cells and nonclassical monocytes. Transcriptional shifts in immune/metabolic pathways confirmed by scRNA-seq.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT with single-cell RNA sequencing

benefits.specificClaim: Systematic evidence of anti-aging effects through mitophagy induction in humans
benefits.replicationStatus: Supported by systematic review of 5 human studies

evidence.title: Targeting aging with urolithin A in humans: A systematic review
evidence.findings: Systematic review of 5 human studies found UA (10-1000 mg/day) showed dose-dependent anti-inflammatory effects, upregulated mitochondrial/autophagy genes, and improved muscle strength and endurance.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review following PRISMA guidelines, 5 eligible studies with 250 participants

benefits.specificClaim: Potential to improve cognitive function via mitophagy and lysosomal restoration
benefits.replicationStatus: Preclinical only — no human cognitive trials published

evidence.title: Urolithin A improves Alzheimer's disease cognition and restores mitophagy and lysosomal functions
evidence.findings: Three AD mouse models showed improved learning, memory, and olfactory function with long-term UA treatment. Reduced amyloid-beta and tau pathology via restored mitophagy and lysosomal function.
evidence.methodology: Three transgenic AD mouse models with behavioral and pathological assessment

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces cartilage degeneration and alleviates joint pain

evidence.title: Urolithin A improves mitochondrial health, reduces cartilage degeneration, and alleviates pain in osteoarthritis
evidence.findings: Improved mitochondrial respiration in chondrocytes from OA donors. In mouse OA models, reduced cartilage degeneration, synovial inflammation, and pain through increased mitophagy.
evidence.methodology: In vitro chondrocyte assays plus mouse OA model

safety.claim: Well-tolerated at doses up to 2000 mg in clinical trials with no serious adverse events

evidence.title: The mitophagy activator urolithin A is safe and induces a molecular signature of improved mitochondrial and cellular health in humans
evidence.findings: First-in-human study demonstrated safety at doses up to 2000 mg. No serious adverse events reported. Mild GI symptoms were the most common side effects.
evidence.methodology: Dose-escalation safety study in healthy elderly volunteers

evidence.title: Targeting aging with urolithin A in humans: A systematic review
evidence.findings: Across 5 human studies (250 participants, 10-1000 mg/day), UA was consistently safe and well-tolerated. No significant adverse events across studies.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of safety data across 5 human trials

safety.claim: No adverse cardiovascular effects; potential HDL-C improvement

evidence.title: Evaluation of Urolithin A Efficacy in Heart Failure Patients with Reduced Ejection Fraction: A Randomized, Double-blind, Crossover, Placebo-controlled Clinical Trial
evidence.findings: In HFrEF patients, UA 500 mg BID for 4 weeks showed no adverse cardiac effects and significantly increased HDL-C (+6.46 mg/dL, p=0.026). No significant cardiac functional improvements at this dose/duration.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, crossover, placebo-controlled trial in heart failure patients

dosage.claim: Optimal dose range established across multiple clinical trials

evidence.title: Effect of Urolithin A Supplementation on Muscle Endurance and Mitochondrial Health in Older Adults: A Randomized Clinical Trial
evidence.findings: 1000 mg/day for 4 months improved muscle endurance and reduced inflammatory biomarkers in older adults mean age 71.7.

evidence.title: Urolithin A improves muscle strength, exercise performance, and biomarkers of mitochondrial health in a randomized trial in middle-aged adults
evidence.findings: Both 500 mg and 1000 mg doses improved muscle strength. 1000 mg showed additional endurance benefits. Both doses reduced inflammatory markers.
